item.title: Life and Death

item.title: Then & Now - Bridger Walker - Hero
item.description: The most deserved belt in history was given to Bridger Walker, the little boy who had rescued his little sister from a German shepherd dog. He had 90 stitches all over his body, but he saved his three-year-old sister from certain death. And he had declared "If someone has to die, it would be me, I'm the older brother" The World Boxing Council ? (WBC) recognized him as a full-time world champion. He holds the official WBC historical record of being the best fighter in the world for one day. Full respect...????
item.dc:creator: mfrybeasley

item.title: Built during the Safavid Empire (1501–1736), the Sheikh Lotfollah Mosque is a triumph of Persian architecture
item.description: The Safavid Empire had an ideal geographic location for trade, with a long coastline between Arabia and India. A major export of the Safavid Empire was its raw silk and silk textiles. Persian carpets were also especially popular in Europe during the modern period.
item.dc:creator: TrytoTry

item.title: Remember These Ignorant Bigots From Yesterday?
item.description: The sign is down and the manager and owners are pointing fingers at each other as to which bigot amongst them was the one to post the sign. Public shaming works. Time to chase all the other bigots back into the closet. https://www.enidnews.com/news/updated-kingfisher-theater-reverses-course-on-same-sex-kissing-scene-in-lightyear/article_f8b5f506-f0d5-11ec-a79e-ab22e52b1093.html

item.title: When you’re fast, you’re not slow dump
item.description:
Seriously hate that
If you haven’t read the email, Google it
I agree Clint,
?
?‍?
It is fun to wear walnut beads during Mardi Gras for this exact reason
!!!
Cousins!!!!
!?!?!
Frick, it is so fricking hawt down here rn
Like totally
Running up that Hill - Sophia Bush
Song ID at top
so interesting oh, Alicia Keys - Falling
Is this fake????!!!!!
Heh
Hakuna Matata
Immediately
Peace out y’all xo
item.dc:creator: TheCrowGirl

item.title: How do you like me now?
item.description: A couple of years ago, an employee who didn't like me for whatever reason waged a campaign against me and tried to get me fired. During the process, my boss implied that I needed to learn to get along better with coworkers and that I was rubbing people the wrong way. After that I did some deep introspection and realized he was right, I was generally angry and abrasive and needed to change. I did some therapy and was put on prozac and after a while just started feeling more relaxed and less anxious and less stressed, which overall resulted in me just being a generally nicer person to everyone around me. Today I had 2 women in stitches because I fixed the problem (firmware update on a docking station) and asked her if she wanted me to replace the personally owned USB hub she was using with a stock one from IT. She asked if it mattered and I said "well if you ever get escorted out of here by security they may not believe that belongs to you" and they both busted up laughing. Boss was walking by and heard the commotion and popped his head in. He said "I oh I should have known it was you. These women are usually quiet as church mice, 5 minutes with you and they're cackling like school girls." and he was laughing too. I'd say I've conquered whatever early impressions he had of me back then. Feels good. ________ pre FP edit: This seems to have wheels so before it hits FP, if it does, I wanted to highlight one important aspect of this story... My boss didn't fire me back then. He gave it to me straight and then gave me an opportunity. This doesn't happen often enough in corporate America and very often good people end up getting tossed aside and feeling dejected and lost. Kudos to him. I don't have much respect for many "bosses", but this guy I do. ______________ Actual FP edit: Wow, thank you so much for the support! I thought I'd get railed as a cuck or some shit lol. I also realize reading this back that I too quickly summarized the initial conflict and made it sound like someone else was to blame for my then predicament. The person that came after me had a legitimate gripe and I tripped over my ego several times in trying to make it right but only made it worse. They are not to blame and actually I am thankful that they put me in the position I was in, because it forced me to ask hard questions and make harder choices.

item.title: It Feel Like Summer
item.description: Every day gets hotter than the one before. Heatwave in the Southern US and 100 °F where I’m at. Stay hydrated folks. Photographer Jamie Beck perfectly captures the sweltering heat of the season with this cinemgraph from her Provence self-portrait series. Summertime soundtrack by Childish Gambino EDIT - Had to delete and repost, something happened to the clip and turned it into a still photo smh
item.dc:creator: AkitaFuriosa
